jokes.text: Due to budget cutbacks, we will no longer be offering free squid guts in the kitchen.
jokes.setup: Announcement about budget cutbacks suggests normal office amenities being cut
jokes.punchline: The 'amenity' being cut is disgusting squid guts
jokes.explanation: Solid world-building humor that establishes the weird universe, economical delivery

jokes.text: Oh, boo-hoo.
jokes.setup: Professor announces loss of free squid guts
jokes.punchline: Bender's sarcastic dismissal shows his callousness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - dismissive and selfish, economical delivery

jokes.text: I like how it's not killing us so far.
jokes.setup: Professor shows off new invention
jokes.punchline: Fry's low bar for success with Professor's inventions
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the Professor's history of dangerous inventions, very Fry-like optimism

jokes.text: You see, as I age, I've been shrinking and feeling colder. So now I need twice as many sweaters, in a smaller size.
jokes.setup: Professor explains his duplication invention
jokes.punchline: The incredibly specific and ridiculous personal need driving scientific advancement
jokes.explanation: Excellent Professor character moment - using advanced science for trivial personal comfort

jokes.text: So, that's where baby sweaters come from.
jokes.setup: Professor demonstrates duplication making two smaller sweaters
jokes.punchline: Fry treats it like learning about biological reproduction
jokes.explanation: Clever play on 'where babies come from' - economical and fits Fry's childlike wonder

jokes.text: The Scary Door opening sequence with eerie canal and two E's joke
jokes.setup: Parody of Twilight Zone opening
jokes.punchline: Pedantic correction about spelling 'eerie' while describing supernatural elements
jokes.explanation: Good parody with unexpected pedantic detail breaking the spooky mood

jokes.text: Dr. Daniel Zenus, an inventor with a terminal case of 'The Lazies.'
jokes.setup: Introduction of character with medical-sounding condition
jokes.punchline: Making laziness sound like a terminal disease
jokes.explanation: Clever medicalization of laziness, sets up the Scary Door segment theme

jokes.text: Dr. Zenus, for a lifetime of scientific achievement, we present this award to your robot.
jokes.setup: Award ceremony for scientific achievement
jokes.punchline: Award goes to the robot who did all the work, not the lazy scientist
jokes.explanation: Good ironic escalation of the laziness theme, logical consequence played straight

jokes.text: Daddy, I love you. (said to robot)
jokes.setup: Child approaches during award ceremony
jokes.punchline: Child says 'I love you' to the robot instead of the father
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation - the robot has completely replaced the father, even emotionally

jokes.text: If only I'd programmed the robot to be more careful what I wished for. Robot, experience this tragic irony for me.
jokes.setup: Character realizes the ironic consequences of his laziness
jokes.punchline: Even experiencing the irony is too much work - delegates that to the robot too
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - character is so lazy he won't even experience his own emotions. Meta-commentary on irony while being ironic.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, do you see a robot in this room named 'Folder'?
jokes.setup: Professor asks Bender to fold sweaters
jokes.punchline: Bender literally interprets request, claiming he's not 'Folder'
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender work-avoidance tactic with literal wordplay, very in-character

jokes.text: Bender, would you mind bending my new sweaters?
jokes.setup: Professor tries to outsmart Bender's literal interpretation
jokes.punchline: Uses 'bending' since that's Bender's name/function
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Bender's escalating laziness complaints, shows Professor trying to be clever

jokes.text: There's only two of them. Wait. You want me to do two things? Man, I'd call my lawyer if dialing a phone wasn't such a hassle.
jokes.setup: Professor mentions there are only two sweaters
jokes.punchline: Bender escalates from complaint about two things to being too lazy to even complain properly
jokes.explanation: Excellent Bender moment - perfect escalation of laziness to absurd degree. The self-defeating nature is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Razza frazza two things.
jokes.setup: Bender continues grumbling about having to do two things
jokes.punchline: Cartoon-style grumbling emphasizes his petulant attitude
jokes.explanation: Extends the two things joke with cartoon grumbling, maintains character consistency

jokes.text: Go to hell, old man! I like your attitude. Let's party.
jokes.setup: Small Bender insults Bender
jokes.punchline: Bender takes the insult as a compliment and wants to be friends
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - he appreciates rudeness and finds kindred spirits in his copies

jokes.text: I'm sorry, do you see a robot in this room named 'Folder'?
jokes.setup: Small Bender is asked to fold sweaters
jokes.punchline: Uses exact same excuse as original Bender
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the copies have the same personality flaws as the original

jokes.text: Does that mean they only do 60% of the work you do, or that they actually do more work because they're only 60% as lazy?
jokes.setup: Bender explains his copies are 60% scale replicas
jokes.punchline: Leela's mathematical joke about Bender's laziness being so extreme that 60% less would be more work
jokes.explanation: Clever mathematical joke about Bender's laziness - good wordplay on scale vs. work ethic

jokes.text: All that and a small wiener? This guy's got it goin' on!
jokes.setup: Reading delivery list with personal care items and regular-sized condom
jokes.punchline: Bender's positive spin on having a small penis
jokes.explanation: Bender's inappropriate positive attitude toward embarrassing situation, economical delivery

jokes.text: I hope that's vanishing cream, 'cause that needs to go away.
jokes.setup: Meeting the ugly space giant customer
jokes.punchline: Bender insults the giant's appearance while playing on vanishing cream
jokes.explanation: Typical Bender insensitivity, decent wordplay but relies on mean-spirited humor

jokes.text: That's cold-blooded!
jokes.setup: Bender's harsh insult to the space giant
jokes.punchline: Fry's shocked reaction to Bender's cruelty
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction from Fry, establishes his moral compass vs Bender's cruelty

jokes.text: Yep, and there's still a little more tension. So, look out!
jokes.setup: Space giant says Bender used humor to defuse tension
jokes.punchline: Bender announces he's about to make things worse
jokes.explanation: Great Bender moment - he can't help but make things worse even when acknowledged for helping

jokes.text: You so ugly, when trick-or-treaters come to your house, they give you candy.
jokes.setup: Classic 'you so ugly' setup
jokes.punchline: Reversal - instead of taking candy, they give it out of pity
jokes.explanation: Clever reversal on the trick-or-treating concept, well-structured insult joke

jokes.text: You so ugly, when you go to the bank, they ask you to put on a ski mask!
jokes.setup: Another 'you so ugly' setup
jokes.punchline: Bank asks him to cover face (opposite of normal bank robber scenario)
jokes.explanation: Good reversal of bank robber trope, solid structure

jokes.text: You so ugly, you cracked The Daily Mirror. It's a newspaper!
jokes.setup: 'You so ugly you crack mirrors' setup
jokes.punchline: Clarification that it's a newspaper, not actual mirror
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - sets up mirror expectation then reveals newspaper pun. Very economical.

jokes.text: Don't feel bad, sir. It's not your fault. You probably just inherited your appearance from your mother.
jokes.setup: Fry tries to comfort the insulted space giant
jokes.punchline: His 'comfort' turns into another insult about his mother
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment - tries to be kind but makes it worse through stupidity. Great misdirection.

jokes.text: God, shield your eyes! It's like Edward James Olmos on IMAX!
jokes.setup: Space giant reveals his angry face
jokes.punchline: Specific pop culture reference to famously craggy-faced actor on large format
jokes.explanation: Very specific and visual joke, good use of IMAX format for emphasis

jokes.text: Did you see his face when I said he so ugly? No, I blacked out, 'cause he so ugly!
jokes.setup: Bender asks if Fry saw the giant's reaction
jokes.punchline: Fry says he blacked out from the ugliness itself
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the ugly theme, Fry joining in the cruelty is character development

jokes.text: One thing each? Sounds fair. Wait, make that four cigars. You want us to do four things?
jokes.setup: Bender asks for two cigars, small Benders agree to one each
jokes.punchline: Bender changes to four, small Benders outraged at doing 'four things'
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the 'two things' running gag, shows all Benders share the laziness

jokes.text: My Manwich!
jokes.setup: Bender units are causing chaos
jokes.punchline: Hermes' specific distress over his canned meat sandwich
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hermes character moment - specific branded food item obsession, very economical

jokes.text: A rat! Yes, she is a rat. No, look!
jokes.setup: Leela complains about Amy not trading lockers, calls her a rat
jokes.punchline: When Amy agrees, Leela clarifies she meant an actual rat
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke, plays on literal vs figurative meaning of 'rat'

jokes.text: Which way to the tiny hookers?
jokes.setup: Tiny Bender appears in the locker room
jokes.punchline: His first question is asking for prostitutes
jokes.explanation: Very Bender-like priorities even at tiny size, economical but relies on shock value

jokes.text: It appears to be giving you the finger, sir. Enjoy.
jokes.setup: Professor asks about tiny Bender in his soup
jokes.punchline: Scruffy's matter-of-fact observation with polite service ending
jokes.explanation: Perfect Scruffy character moment - deadpan professionalism in absurd situation

jokes.text: Help me, help me! I'm too lazy to escape!
jokes.setup: Tiny Bender is in soup and in danger
jokes.punchline: His laziness prevents him from saving himself
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - even in mortal peril, Bender's laziness dominates

jokes.text: Don't wait for me. It's non-convergent! Oh, dip!
jokes.setup: Professor analyzing mathematical series
jokes.punchline: Academic concern followed by casual exclamation
jokes.explanation: Good Professor moment mixing scientific jargon with casual slang

jokes.text: Wait, does that include me?
jokes.setup: Professor says they need to destroy 2,046 Benders
jokes.punchline: Bender's self-interested concern about being included
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender self-interest - more concerned about himself than the crisis

jokes.text: Too bad he didn't have his own Bible.
jokes.setup: Bender gets destroyed after asking if count includes him
jokes.punchline: Fry's matter-of-fact observation about Bender's lack of religious protection
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Bender's death, implies religion might have saved him

jokes.text: Wait, this isn't a real Bender. It's a talking doll from when Bender had that sitcom about the city robot who moves back to the farm.
jokes.setup: They think they found the last Bender
jokes.punchline: It's merchandise from Bender's fictional TV show with ridiculous premise
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about Bender having a sitcom, plays on fish-out-of-water trope

jokes.text: Shut your pumpkin, bumpkin.
jokes.setup: The doll is from Bender's farm-themed sitcom
jokes.punchline: Rural-themed insult using farm vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Clever farm-themed version of Bender's usual insults, good wordplay

jokes.text: Bite my tiny metal ass!
jokes.setup: Tiny Benders appear as gray goo
jokes.punchline: Modified version of Bender's catchphrase for his new size
jokes.explanation: Good adaptation of Bender's catchphrase to fit the tiny size situation

jokes.text: God! We've opened Pandora's fly. They'll reproduce without limit, consuming all the matter in the world! Like the Kardashians.
jokes.setup: Professor realizes the magnitude of the crisis
jokes.punchline: Malapropism 'Pandora's fly' and comparison to reality TV family
jokes.explanation: Good malapropism and pop culture reference, though Kardashian joke feels predictable

jokes.text: A problem that solves itself? That sounds like a job for me, Bender.
jokes.setup: Professor says it's a problem that will solve itself
jokes.punchline: Bender volunteers for a job that requires no work
jokes.explanation: Excellent Bender character moment - only volunteers when no work is required

jokes.text: I can no longer face my children!
jokes.setup: News reports consequences are minimal except for hardened alcoholics
jokes.punchline: News anchor admits to being an alcoholic on air
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about alcoholism, good irony of news anchor being the example

jokes.text: It's like fine cognac with a hint of aged scrotum.
jokes.setup: Professor tastes the alcohol-transformed bathwater
jokes.punchline: Sophisticated wine description ending with disgusting body part
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of refined wine tasting with crude anatomy reference

jokes.text: No! What will I mix with my scotch?
jokes.setup: Professor warns there will be no fresh water on Earth
jokes.punchline: Bender's only concern is cocktail mixing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender priorities - apocalyptic scenario reduced to personal drinking concerns

jokes.text: That's right, Linda. Water is now booze, and everyone's tiddy much protally fit-shaced.
jokes.setup: News report about water turning to alcohol
jokes.punchline: Morbo is drunk and slurring his words in a specific pattern
jokes.explanation: Creative drunk speech pattern, good character moment for normally articulate Morbo

jokes.text: There were no survivors.
jokes.setup: Reporting on Indy 500 race while everyone's drunk
jokes.punchline: Casual delivery of total fatality count
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered matter-of-factly, economical but effective

jokes.text: You want to see a picture of my boy? Sure. That's your penis. That's my boy.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg offers to show picture of his boy
jokes.punchline: It's a picture of his genitals, which he's proud of
jokes.explanation: Relies heavily on shock value and crudeness, though fits Zoidberg's cluelessness

jokes.text: Minds? What?
jokes.setup: Someone struggles to say 'minds' while drunk
jokes.punchline: Fry can't understand the word even when sober
jokes.explanation: Good Fry stupidity joke - he's so dumb he can't understand a simple word

jokes.text: You're so stinky, you need Right Guard and left guard.
jokes.setup: Following up on the smell theme
jokes.punchline: Plays on deodorant brand name to suggest needing double protection
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay on brand name, solid insult joke structure

jokes.text: Your face has been declared a weapon of mass disgusting.
jokes.setup: Continuing to insult the space giant
jokes.punchline: Plays on 'weapon of mass destruction' with 'disgusting'
jokes.explanation: Solid political reference adaptation, good wordplay structure

jokes.text: You mean you'll do two things, and I only have to do one thing? Yeah. Just save the world. Sucker.
jokes.setup: Fry offers to fold sweaters if Bender saves the world
jokes.punchline: Bender thinks he got the better deal despite saving the world being harder
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Bender's lazy logic makes him think world-saving is easier than folding

jokes.text: If we all pitch in, we each only have to do one quintillionth of a thing.
jokes.setup: Bender needs to motivate the legion of mini-Benders
jokes.punchline: Breaks down the one task mathematically to make it sound easier
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback to the work complaint theme, mathematical precision makes it funnier

jokes.text: Let this beating be a lesson about never attacking those more handsome than oneself.
jokes.setup: Giant Bender is beating up the space monster
jokes.punchline: Bender's self-aggrandizing moral lesson
jokes.explanation: Good Bender narcissism - turns violence into moral superiority based on looks

jokes.text: Your mama's so ugly! I told you not to talk about my mama!
jokes.setup: Space monster mentions his mama
jokes.punchline: Bender can't resist the setup even though it triggers the monster's rage
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the mama insult that started the conflict, Bender can't help himself

jokes.text: Man, do I have to walk?
jokes.setup: Bender is told to walk toward the light (dying)
jokes.punchline: Even in death, he complains about having to do something
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - maintains his laziness even facing death. Excellent dark comedy.

jokes.text: Yeah, if you're into gray dust.
jokes.setup: Space monster says 'At last I'm beautiful!'
jokes.punchline: Bender's unimpressed assessment of being reduced to ash
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response to death, Bender's casual dismissal of destruction

jokes.text: Ladies and gentlemen, if, together we were able to defeat this giant space monster, think how also we might defeat the monsters of poverty and disease and unliteracy.
jokes.setup: Bender gives inspirational speech about working together
jokes.punchline: Says 'unliteracy' instead of 'illiteracy' while advocating education
jokes.explanation: Good ironic malapropism - makes literacy error while promoting literacy

jokes.text: Come on, let's go someplace where we don't have to do one quintillionth of a thing all the time.
jokes.setup: Bender tries to inspire the mini-Benders to keep working
jokes.punchline: They reject even the tiny fraction of work and leave
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback - even the mathematically tiny amount of work is too much for Benders

jokes.text: Maybe I did, Fry. Maybe I did. Or maybe I rescued one last mini-Bender to fold stupid, ugly sweaters while I laughed at him.
jokes.setup: Fry asks if Bender learned about not being lazy
jokes.punchline: Bender's ambiguous response suggests he learned nothing and enslaved a mini-Bender
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - suggests Bender learned nothing and found an even lazier solution. Great character consistency.
